title: phyla nodiflora var. rosea
intro: Phyla nodiflora var. rosea, also known as Wild Algerian tea or Rosea Lippia, is a perennial plant of the family Verbenaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. The herbal preparations of Phyla nodiflora var. rosea include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
